metadata.title: Record It: Zoom (fast) vs Remotion/Brutalist (standout)
metadata.playlist: Sell the Vision — Your Investor Pitch Video
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 7 brief — Video Pitch to Investors: recording the pitch (Zoom path vs Remotion/Brutalist path)

beats.narration_text: Hola — Liam, Episode 6 of 7. You have 10 slides drafted and a business case. Now you need a video. This episode covers two valid paths: the fast one that gets you the 80% in an afternoon, and the branded-video one that puts you in the top 25% of presentations. Neither is required — the assignment accepts both. The choice depends on your time, your technical comfort, and your goal. If your pitch is solid and your deadline is tight, the fast path is the right call. If you want to stand out and you are comfortable with code, the quality path is the one assignment in INFO 7375 where the production toolkit you have been building is directly available as your production tool.

props.command: Record one timed take of my full pitch. If I'm over 8 minutes or reading from slides, that's the note for take two.
props.runningText: two valid paths — fast path earns the 80%, quality path competes for top 25%. The choice is yours…
output: Fast path: Zoom screen-share + record — totally acceptable, earns the 80%
Quality path: Remotion / Loom / Descript — production quality, top 25% tier
Requirements either way: under 8 min · face visible · slides visible · clear audio

beats.narration_text: The fast path is Zoom. Open your slides, start a Zoom meeting with yourself, share your screen, hit record, and present. You can record locally or to the cloud. If you use Google Slides, you can also use the built-in presentation recording feature. If you use PowerPoint, the recording mode works the same way. The whole setup takes under five minutes. This path is completely acceptable and earns the 80% if your content is strong. What the grader checks on the Zoom path: the slides are visible, your voice is clear, and your face appears at some point — even briefly at the beginning or end. That is it. The Zoom path is the right call if your pitch is solid, your deadline is close, and you would rather spend your remaining time rehearsing than setting up a production pipeline. Strong content delivered clearly on Zoom beats polished production with a weak pitch every time.

props.artifactHeading: Totally acceptable · earns the 80% on strong content
artifactLines: Setup: Zoom meeting with yourself · share screen · record locally or to cloud. Under 5 minutes.
Alternatives: Google Slides built-in recording · PowerPoint presentation mode.
Grader checks: slides visible · clear voice · face appears at some point (even briefly).
Right call when: pitch is solid · deadline is close · time better spent rehearsing.
Strong content on Zoom beats polished production with a weak pitch — every time.
props.sparkLine: Zoom earns the 80% if your content is strong. Don't over-invest in production over clarity.

beats.act: the quality path — Loom, Descript, Remotion
beats.narration_text: The quality path has three tools at different levels of effort. Loom is the easiest upgrade from Zoom: it records your screen and webcam simultaneously, adds a clean interface around the recording, and gives you a shareable link. It takes five minutes to set up and produces a noticeably cleaner-looking video than a raw Zoom recording. Descript goes one level further: it records, transcribes, and lets you edit by editing the transcript — cut filler words, re-record over mistakes, add captions. If you have the time, Descript gets you close to a polished final product without advanced video skills. Remotion is the technical path for students comfortable with code. It is a React video framework — you compose your pitch as React components, animate slide transitions, add captions, and export a proper branded MP4. The quality signal it sends is different from the other two paths: a Remotion video looks like something a founder made, not something a student recorded. That signal matters in a pitch.

props.artifactHeading: Loom · Descript · Remotion — three levels of effort
artifactLines: Loom: screen + webcam recording, clean interface, shareable link. 5 min setup. Easiest upgrade.
Descript: record → transcribe → edit by editing text. Cut filler, re-record mistakes, add captions.
Remotion: React video framework. Compose pitch as components, animate, export branded MP4.
Remotion signal: looks like a founder made it, not a student recorded it.
Choose based on time and technical comfort — all three are valid quality-path options.
props.sparkLine: Remotion looks like a founder made it. That signal matters in a pitch.

beats.narration_text: For students who have been working through this series, there is a third path: the Brutalist-art toolkit. If you followed along with the A5B and A4 build work, you already have a Remotion setup on your machine. The toolkit's `deck-lecture` and `brutalist-slides` skills are designed to turn a slide deck into a branded video with smooth transitions, the Claude palette, caption support, and a clean outro. This is the excellence-tier path — it takes more setup time than Loom or Zoom, but it produces a video that looks like a product. A branded pitch video signals investor-readiness: this person built something that looks like something. The meta-loop closes here: the Conductor Brief you wrote in A5, the brand identity from A6, the tool architecture from A5B — all of it becomes content in this video. The pitch video is not a separate assignment. It is the proof that all of the work in the semester adds up to something an investor would actually watch. Remotion is the tool that makes that visual.

props.artifactHeading: Excellence tier — the one assignment where the toolkit is your tool
artifactLines: If you have a Remotion setup from A5B/A4 work, you already have this toolkit.
`deck-lecture` + `brutalist-slides`: slide deck → branded video with Claude palette + captions.
Signal: a branded video looks like a product. It says investor-readiness without saying it.
Meta-loop: Conductor Brief (A5) · brand identity (A6) · architecture (A5B) → all become pitch content.
The pitch video is the proof that the semester's work adds up. Remotion makes that visual.
props.sparkLine: The pitch video is the proof that the semester adds up. Remotion makes that visual.

beats.act: requirements either way + the 8-minute rehearsal law
beats.narration_text: Whatever path you take, four requirements are non-negotiable. One: under 8 minutes. This is the hardest requirement for most students. The first timed take is usually between 9 and 11 minutes. That means you are not done practicing — you are finding where to cut. Record yourself. Time yourself. Edit your script until the full pitch consistently hits under 8 minutes before you record the final take. Over 8 minutes loses points. Rehearse until it is automatic. Two: face visible at some point. You do not need to be on camera the whole time. One moment is enough — the intro or the close. Three: slides visible during the pitch. Screen share, embedded slides, picture-in-picture — any format where the investor can see what you are referring to. Four: clear audio. Test your microphone before recording. Find a quiet room. Use headphones to prevent echo. Bad audio is the single most common reason a grader stops watching early — it is a friction you create before the content even lands. Check the audio in the first 10 seconds of your recording before committing to the full take.

artifactLines: 1. Under 8 minutes. First take is usually 9–11 min. Record → cut → rehearse until automatic.
2. Face visible at some point. Intro or close — one moment is enough.
3. Slides visible. Screen share, PiP, embedded — any format where the investor sees what you're referencing.
4. Clear audio. Test mic before recording. Quiet room. Headphones prevent echo.
Bad audio is why graders stop watching early — it blocks the content before it lands. Check the first 10 seconds.
props.sparkLine: Rehearse until 8 minutes is automatic. Over time loses points.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Episode 6. Two paths, one goal: a clear, confident pitch video under 8 minutes. Fast path: Zoom with screen share — totally acceptable, earns the 80% on strong content, works in an afternoon. Quality path: Loom for a quick upgrade, Descript for editing control, Remotion plus the Brutalist-art toolkit for the excellence tier. Requirements on either path: under 8 minutes, face visible, slides visible, clear audio. The 8-minute rule is the one you will under-estimate. Record your first take. Time it. Expect it to be over. That is the note. Rehearse until it is not. Over time means lost points, and no production quality compensates for a pitch that runs long.

artifactLines: Fast path: Zoom + screen share. Acceptable, earns 80% on strong content. Done in an afternoon.
Quality path: Loom (quick upgrade) → Descript (editing) → Remotion + Brutalist-art (excellence tier).
4 non-negotiables: under 8 min · face visible · slides visible · clear audio.
8-minute law: first take will be over. Record it. Time it. Cut. Rehearse until automatic.
No production quality compensates for a pitch that runs long.
Strong content on Zoom beats polished production with a weak pitch.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. One action, no prompts needed for this episode. Record one complete timed take of your full pitch today. Use whatever setup you have — Zoom, Loom, or a phone propped on a stack of books. Just record it once, all the way through, without stopping. Then do two things. First: time it. Open the recording and note the total length. If it is over 8 minutes, you have found your single most important edit. Go back to your script and cut. The 8-minute constraint will tell you which sections are filler and which are essential. Second: watch the first 60 seconds without sound. Does your face appear? Are your slides visible? Is the frame well-lit? These are the production checks. Then watch the last 60 seconds with sound and no slides. Is your ask clear? Does it land with confidence? If you stumble on the problem or the ask — those are the two most important moments — rewrite and re-record. The first timed take is never the final take. It is the diagnostic.

props.command: Record one complete timed take of your full pitch — Zoom, Loom, or whatever you have. Then: (1) time it — if over 8 min, that's the edit; (2) watch first 60 sec without sound — face visible, slides visible, well-lit; (3) watch last 60 sec with sound only — is the ask clear and confident? The first timed take is the diagnostic, not the final take.
props.runningText: no prompt needed this episode — record one take, time it, find the cut. The 8-minute constraint is the editing note…

beats.narration_text: Record It: Zoom (fast) vs Remotion/Brutalist (standout). Liam, in for Bear.
